verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is a soluble protein localized to the acrosomal lumen (an intra-acrosomal/intracellular compartment). It is not anchored to the plasma membrane by its own mechanism (no TM, GPI, or covalent tethering). While it participates in sperm-egg fusion biology, it does so as an intracellular protein, not as a surface-accessible protein body. The protein is not directly accessible from the extracellular face.
